raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 577 aims to protect the rights of patients with serious illnesses such as cancer and rheumatoid arthritis.",
    "The legislation puts medical decisions back in the hands of physicians and their patients.",
    "The bill protects the right of doctors to administer and dispense vital medications directly to their patients when medically necessary.",
    "AB 577 is designed to strengthen patient safety, preserve continuity of care, and ensure timely, personalized treatment.",
    "Health plans, insurers, and pharmacy benefit managers in California are increasingly imposing policies that prevent doctors from providing necessary treatments directly in their offices.",
    "These restrictions force patients to travel to off-site infusion centers or wait for delayed deliveries.",
    "AB 577 restores the role of the physician in determining the most appropriate and safest location for patient care.",
    "The bill ensures patient consent and cost transparency.",
    "AB 577 limits the scope of care to in-network providers while exempting hospital outpatient facilities.",
    "The bill is aimed at safeguarding the vital relationship between patients and their healthcare providers."
  ]
}
```
